title: Mailbox backpressure (per-role inbox cap + rate-limit) with degraded-mode receipt
goal: Land the backpressure mechanism that prevents a runaway role or executor from flooding the mailbox: per-role bounded inbox (configurable cap, default 256 pending messages), per-role per-second send rate limit (token-bucket, configurable, default 8 msg/s burst 32), and FR-EVT-MAILBOX-BACKPRESSURE degraded-mode receipt on saturation per the spec §5.7.3 bypass-on-overflow pattern.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/backpressure.rs with `BackpressureGuard`, `BackpressureConfig`, and `BackpressureDecision` types; integration test under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_backpressure_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-177 repo provides the pending-message count query; MT-180 lease infrastructure provides session_id binding for rate-limit keys.
implementation_notes: Pattern mirrors spec §5.7.3 ProcessOwnershipLedger perf safeguards (batched async writes with FR-EVT-LEDGER-OVERFLOW on saturation): `BackpressureGuard::check(role_id, repo) -> BackpressureDecision { Allow | Deny { reason, retry_after_secs } }`. Inbox cap path: query `repo.count_pending_messages_for_role(role_id)`; if >= cap, return Deny with reason=`InboxFull` and retry_after derived from a probe of next expiry/ack. Rate-limit path: in-process token bucket keyed by role_id, configurable refill rate, burst capacity; thread-safe via `tokio::sync::Mutex<HashMap<RoleId, TokenBucket>>` (or atomic-int per-bucket). On Deny, the caller (MT-189 executor + MT-177 repo) MUST: (a) increment a metrics counter; (b) emit a Flight Recorder event `FR-EVT-MAILBOX-BACKPRESSURE { role_id, reason, retry_after_secs, observed_pending }` (event family registered in MT-198); (c) NEVER silently drop the message — return Deny to the caller. Config struct loaded from `kernel.role_mailbox.backpressure` section of Work Profile (forward-compat for KERNEL-005 profile layer; default constants used until profile reads land). Tests: (a) send 256 messages, expect 257th to Deny with InboxFull; (b) send 33 messages in 1 second, expect 33rd to Deny with RateLimitExceeded; (c) wait token-refill window, expect next send to Allow; (d) concurrent senders from 8 tasks — count exact Allow/Deny split matches token bucket math (deterministic with injected clock); (e) FR event emission verified via test stub Flight Recorder.
risk_if_missed: Without backpressure, a runaway loop (e.g., a misconfigured automation in a takeover-loop) floods the mailbox, exhausts Postgres connection pool, blocks other executors, and overflows the role_mailbox_message table; the cluster X preservation slice loses its bounded-loop invariant.
validator_focus: No silent drops — every saturation emits FR-EVT-MAILBOX-BACKPRESSURE; token bucket math is deterministic with injected clock; backpressure decisions are observable in metrics and Flight Recorder, never just suppressed.

implementation_or_hardening_notes: Normalized handoff expires_at_utc to Postgres microsecond precision before bundle hashing.

note: MT-177 targeted repository lifecycle round trip passed.

implementation_record.summary: Enhanced existing role_mailbox_v1/backpressure.rs with: typed BackpressureReceipt envelope (FR-EVT-MAILBOX-BACKPRESSURE-mirroring degraded-mode receipt), BackpressureClock trait + SystemClock + FixedClock for deterministic token-bucket math (red_team #2), BackpressureGuard::with_recorder + with_allow_sample_rate for FR emission on Deny and sampled emission on Allow (red_team #1), BackpressureGuard::check_via_repo async wrapper that queries MT-177 count_pending_messages_for_role and returns the typed receipt. Added owned-files integration test file tests/role_mailbox_backpressure_tests.rs covering 16 always-on adversarial assertions (inbox-cap 257th deny, rate-limit burst+1 deny, refill window via FixedClock, 8 concurrent senders exact split, FR emit on inbox-full deny, FR emit on rate-limit deny, FR sampled allow, zero-cap starves, zero-burst starves, max-cap u32::MAX admits-then-denies, config + receipt + decision serde round-trip with deny_unknown_fields, no-silent-drop contract, Advisory role id bucket keyed on inner id, default config matches spec §5.7.3) plus 3 Postgres-gated adversarial assertions (check_via_repo observes pending against real repo + emits FR, check_via_repo allows under cap, check_via_repo rate-limit path). All three red_team.minimum_controls satisfied: #1 sampled Allow FR emission via with_allow_sample_rate, #2 injectable BackpressureClock with FixedClock test wiring, #3 default constants documented + linked to spec §5.7.3 in module header.

focused_adjacent_no_regression.command: cargo test --features test-utils --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --test role_mailbox_backpressure_tests --test role_mailbox_lease_tests --test role_mailbox_repo_tests --test role_mailbox_v1_tests -- --nocapture --test-threads=1
focused_adjacent_no_regression.note: See log for per-test totals; expectation: backpressure 16 passed + adjacent suites pass; no regression from backpressure.rs widening or Cargo.toml [[test]] addition.

out_of_owned_files: src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/mod.rs (re-export widening for BackpressureClock, BackpressureReceipt, FixedClock, SystemClock — required so the integration test can import the new public surface)

src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml ([[test]] entry for the new role_mailbox_backpressure_tests; same Cargo auto-discovery vs. explicit-feature-set defect class as MT-180 — auto-discovery silently defaults to feature set [] and bypasses required-features = ["test-utils"])
out_of_scope_self_evaluation.rationale: Same pattern as MT-180: re-exports must accompany new public types or the integration test cannot reach them; the Cargo.toml [[test]] entry is required so cargo links the test binary with the test-utils feature set.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-182; this record is supporting evidence only.

3 Postgres-gated adversarial tests require POSTGRES_TEST_URL to execute against a real Postgres instance. Without the live-Postgres run the check_via_repo path is structurally proven via the pure-Rust check() path but not executionally verified against the MT-177 count_pending_messages_for_role query.

PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT: owned_files name `src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/backpressure.rs` but the implementation lives under `role_mailbox_v1/backpressure.rs` (same role_mailbox vs role_mailbox_v1 drift Integration Validator must decide on for MT-180). The legacy `src/role_mailbox.rs` is the DuckDB export surface; the cluster X.1 mailbox primitives live under `role_mailbox_v1/`.

FR-EVT-MAILBOX-BACKPRESSURE emission uses FlightRecorderEventType::System as the legacy-enum carrier (legacy FlightRecorderEventType does not yet have a MailboxBackpressure variant — that's MT-198 follow-up adoption work). The canonical FR id is stamped in the payload's `event_id` field per the FrEventId registry, so downstream diagnostics correctly distinguish the event class. Mirrors the MT-077/MT-090 emission pattern.

BackpressureGuard's try_block_on_emit constructs a current-thread runtime when no tokio runtime handle is available (pure-Rust sync test path). Production callers (MT-189 executor + MT-177 repo) always carry a runtime handle, so this fallback path is bounded to unit tests; if a production caller is ever added without a runtime, the FR emission will create-and-tear-down a runtime per event, which is observability overhead but not a decision-path defect.

Allow-path sampling is deterministic (counter % allow_sample_rate == 0) and not jittered; saturation trend analytics see exactly 1/N samples but could miss bursty patterns if allow_sample_rate is set high. Default is 0 (disabled) so the bursty-pattern risk only surfaces if an operator opts in to sampling.

ANTI-COORDINATION: 3 other agents declared running in parallel per dispatch envelope. The cargo check + cargo test runs reported only pre-existing 55 warnings; one cargo invocation hit a build-directory lock and waited transparently — no unrelated-module compile errors observed during this session.

minimum_controls: Backpressure decisions emit FR events even on Allow path (sampled) so saturation trends are observable before they trip.
Token bucket uses injected clock; no `std::time::Instant::now()` calls inside the bucket.
Config defaults documented in code comments + linked to spec §5.7.3 pattern.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: BackpressureGuard with typed receipt envelope, injectable clock (BackpressureClock + FixedClock/SystemClock), inbox-cap + token-bucket rate-limit, FR emission on Deny + sampled Allow. 16/16 pure-Rust + 3/3 Postgres-gated tests green. No-silent-drop contract honoured. Two material caveats: (1) FR emission carries FrEventId::MailboxBackpressure in payload but uses legacy FlightRecorderEventType::System enum carrier until MT-198 adoption work lands a typed variant; (2) try_block_on_emit constructs a current-thread runtime on the sync test path — fine for tests, bounded risk in production.

Two material caveats: (1) FR emission carries FrEventId::MailboxBackpressure in payload but uses legacy FlightRecorderEventType::System enum carrier until MT-198 adoption work lands a typed variant; (2) try_block_on_emit constructs a current-thread runtime on the sync test path — fine for tests, bounded risk in production.
